Health and Well-being Chapter 1 Introduction to SDG 3

Health and Well-being Lizzie Kriz 2026-04-09 Chapter 1 Introduction to SDG 3 The UN’s third sustainable development goal is to ensure the healthy lives and promote well-being for all at all ages(UN). This encompasses alot of issues, here are a few challenges the UN had chosen to focus on (WHO): Maternal mortality Child mortality Infectious diseases Noncommunicable diseases Substance abuse Much of this SDG’s focus is on Diseases that have historically devastated regions such as malaria, tuberculosis, and recovering the Covid epidemic falls under this umbrella as well. (Taikan et al, 2022) But it also focuses on epidemics that are emerging and are serious concerns when setting the standards for the developing world.

Chapter 2 Obesity My project will focus on Noncommunicable diseases which are chronic, slow-progressing conditions NOT passed between people, like cardiovascular diseases, cancer, obesity and diabetes. These diseases are responsible for over 74% of global deaths and significantly impact individuals’ quality of life (WHO). These ailments plague all countries in some way, but are particularly concerning because of their prevalence in developed countries(CDC)- it seems that many of these sicknesses may be the effect of new problems arising as we adapt and change our lifestyles and diet. Of these diseases, I would like to focus on obesity, it seems important to understand how it takes hold in places with good food security and healthcare systems to find a way to stop the spread of a new kind of disease in societies that should set the standard. Obesity also has an arguably more powerful impact of devloping countries because of the “double burden” of disease where both malnutrition and obesity are prevalent in weak healthcare systems (WHO)

Chapter 3 Avenues of exploration There are two main suspected factors of obesity (CDC): Poor exercise and/or + Poor diet However studies find that ‘you can’t outrun a bad diet’ (Godoy, 2025) meaning the main contributor of this condition is controlled by the food market. This combined with the fact that obesity is a modern disease must mean that the cause was introduced fairly recently. Here are some factors we will examine in relation to Obesity to investigate further (Our world on data): Diet (ratio of what foods) GDP Historical change Lifestyle Daily Calories available/consumed
